zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Giffard於大約1224年出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾波頓時，她的父親Hugh de Gifford31歲，母親Sybil de Cormeilles33歲。她於從1239年1月8日到1240年1月7日與William Devereux Lord of Lyonshall在英國英格蘭賀福爾賀福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1297年8月20日在英國賀福爾享年74歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda de Giffard于大约1224年出生在英国英格兰維爾爾波頓时，她的父亲Hugh de Gifford31岁，母亲Sybil de Cormeilles33岁。她于从1239年1月8日到1240年1月7日与William Devereux Lord of Lyonshall在英国英格兰賀福爾賀福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1297年8月20日在英国賀福爾享年74岁，埋葬在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Giffard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: (i) relationship name from the Continental Germanic personal name Gifard. (ii) nickname, perhaps sometimes from Old French giffard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Guiver
meanings: English: (i) nickname, apparently from Middle English giver ‘giver, donor, bestower’. (ii) variant of Giffard with voicing of /f/ to /v/ and loss of final -d.

variants.name: Gafford
meanings: English (southeastern): assimilated form of Gatford, a name of various origins. In Sussex and Surrey it is perhaps a variant of the habitational name Catford from a place so called in Somerset. Gatford is also from the habitational names Gateford (in Worksop, Nottinghamshire) or Gateforth (near Selby, Yorkshire), both formed with Old English gāt ‘goat’ + ford ‘ford’, influenced by Old Norse geit ‘goat’.

Probably also an altered form of German Gaffert: habitational name from Gaffert near Köslin, Brandenburg, or from a personal name formed with Middle High German gate ‘fellow, companion’. The surname Gaffert is very rare in Germany.

English: perhaps an altered form of Gatewood , an occupational name for a ‘goatherd’ (Middle English gate-werde, Old English gātweard) or for a ‘gate keeper’ (Middle English gateward, Old English ʒeatweard, dialect gātward).

variants.name: Gifford
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Giffard was born about 1224, in Boyton, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Hugh de Gifford, was 31 and her mother, Sybil de Cormeilles, was 33. She married William Devereux Lord of Lyonshall from 8 January 1239 to 7 January 1240, in Hereford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 20 August 1297, in Herefordshire, England, at the age of 74, and was buried in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé William Devereux Lord of Lyonshall du 8 janvier 1239 au 7 janvier 1240, à Hereford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 20 août 1297, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterrée à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lei sposato William Devereux Lord of Lyonshall da 8 gennaio 1239 a 7 gennaio 1240, nel Hereford, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Matilda de Giffardが約1224年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアボイトンで生まれた時，父Hugh de Giffordは31歳，母Sybil de Cormeillesは33歳であった。から1239年1月8日まで1240年1月7日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアハーエフォードでWilliam Devereux Lord of Lyonshallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1297年8月20日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで74歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターにて埋葬された。

ko.Evesham All Saints with St Lawrence, Wor: 영국 워스스터실르 에베샤 앨 세인츠 위스 스트 로렌스
ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Giffard(이)가 대략 1224년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 보이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh de Gifford(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sybil de Cormeilles(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1239년 1월 8일부터 1240년 1월 7일까지 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 허퍼드에서 William Devereux Lord of Lyonshall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1297년 8월 20일 영국 허퍼드실르에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 워스스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Giffard nasceu aproximadamente 1224, em Boyton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Hugh de Gifford, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Sybil de Cormeilles, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com William Devereux Lord of Lyonshall de 8 de janeiro de 1239 a 7 de janeiro de 1240, em Hereford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 20 de agosto de 1297, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 74 anos, e foi sepultada em Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Evesham All Saints with St Lawrence, Wor: эвешам алл сеинц вит ст ларенс, ворсестиршир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda de Giffard родилась около 1224 г. в местности боитон, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Hugh de Gifford было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Sybil de Cormeilles, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Devereux Lord of Lyonshall с 8 января 1239 по 7 января 1240 в местности хирефорд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 20 августа 1297 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 74 лет и была похоронена в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
